commands.review_summary:
The PR wires the Control UI Chat header session picker to the existing `switchChatSession` handler and adds an app-level regression test for selecting another chat from that picker.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +62. Total +62 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main renders the Chat header picker without the switch handler while picker option clicks depend on that injected callback. I did not run a live browser reproduction in this read-only review.

target.title: [codex] fix ControlUI session picker switching
target.body:
Fixes #87984.

## Summary

- Wire the Chat page header session picker to the existing `switchChatSession` handler.
- Add an app-render regression test that opens the header picker, selects another session, and verifies the active session changes.

## Root Cause

The Chat header rendered `renderChatSessionSelect(state)` without passing the switch callback. The picker option click path depends on the injected callback, and the component default is a no-op, so clicking a right-side picker option only closed the picker instead of changing the active chat.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Selecting another conversation from the Control UI Chat header session picker now routes through the same `switchChatSession` path used by the left sidebar.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with the UI Vitest/jsdom harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The new regression test renders the Chat page, opens the header session picker, clicks the `agent:main:work` option, and observes `state.sessionKey` plus persisted settings changing to `agent:main:work`.

Observed result after fix: Both UI shards passed: `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts` 6/6 and `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` 61/61.

What was not tested: I did not run a live browser session against a real gateway; the proof uses the existing Control UI render tests around the affected click path.
